commit.message:
fix: Add CDN fallback for Phaser.js loading

Fixes #1549

Problem:
- In Docker deployment, phaser.min.js doesn't exist because npm install
  doesn't run (Dockerfile only copies static files)
- This causes 404 errors and blank office canvas

Solution:
- Add onerror handler to phaser script tag that falls back to CDN
- Uses jsDelivr CDN with correct version (3.90.0) matching package.json
- Fallback is transparent - logs to console but doesn't interrupt load

Impact:
- Office canvas now works even if phaser.min.js is missing
- Development still uses local file for performance
- Production gets CDN fallback for reliability

commit.message:
feat: battery sysfs fix + full openrazer device parity (26 new devices)

## Battery Fix — BlackWidow V3 Mini HyperSpeed Wireless (PID 0x0271)

Root cause: the razerkbd kernel module was already bound to the dongle's
interface 3. Synaptix calling claim_interface(3) detached it, breaking the
wireless channel and causing STATUS_TIMEOUT on every battery query.

Fix: added sysfs-first battery reading in usb_backend.rs that reads
charge_level and charge_status from /sys/bus/hid/devices/ exposed by
the razerkbd kernel module. libusb control transfer is kept as fallback
only when sysfs is unavailable (non-razerkbd devices).

Strategy for dongle+wired pair: prefer wired companion sysfs first
(PID 0x0258), fall back to dongle sysfs (PID 0x0271), then USB.
Fixed charge_status validation: status_raw == 1 only (>1 = raw current).

## Device Parity — 26 New Devices

Added DeviceType::Mousepad and DeviceType::Accessory to registry.rs.
All PIDs verified against _reference_openrazer/driver/razeraccessory_driver.h.

Mousepads (8): Firefly HyperFlux (0x0068), Firefly/V2/V2 Pro
  (0x0C00/0x0C04/0x0C08), Goliathus Chroma/Extended/3XL
  (0x0C01/0x0C02/0x0C06), Strider Chroma (0x0C05)

Audio — Nommo speakers (2): Nommo Chroma (0x0517), Nommo Pro (0x0518)
  — excluded from Sidetone/Microphone (headset-only capabilities)

Accessories — Dock/Peripheral (4): Mouse Dock (0x007E), Mouse Dock Pro
  (0x00A4), Mouse Bungee V3 Chroma (0x0F1D), Charging Pad Chroma (0x0F26)

  Base Station V2 Chroma (0x0F20)

commit.message:
fix: validate status in updateAgentStatus, add 'active' to whitelist (#1490)

updateAgentStatus() was setting agent.status directly without calling
validateAgentStatus(), and 'active' was missing from VALID_AGENT_STATUSES.
This caused silent status corruption during interaction pattern restoration:

1. Agent set to 'active' via updateAgentStatus (bypassed validation)
2. Interaction pattern saved previousStatus: 'active'
3. endInteractionPattern called validateAgentStatus('active') → 'idle' (fallback)
4. Agent silently demoted, agent-departing event never fired

Fix:
- Add 'active' to VALID_AGENT_STATUSES (legitimate lifecycle status)
- Call validateAgentStatus() in updateAgentStatus() before assignment
- Use validatedStatus consistently for event checks and return value

Refs: #1490, #1478, #1454

commit.message:
fix: add initial battery query at startup for wireless keyboard

The BlackWidow V3 Mini HyperSpeed (Wireless) was registered with
BatteryState::Unknown at daemon startup, meaning:
  - The UI showed '?' for up to 60 seconds (first polling tick)
  - The system tray never received a TrayUpdate until the first
    successful poll, so the keyboard never appeared in the menu

Fix: mirror the Cobra Pro startup pattern — query battery before
D-Bus registration with up to 3 retries (500ms apart). The result
(real state, or Unknown after 3 failures) is:
  1. Written into the DeviceManager so the first D-Bus get_devices
     call returns real data instead of Unknown
  2. Pushed to TrayUpdate immediately so the keyboard appears in
     the system tray from the moment the daemon starts

The 60s polling loop continues unchanged for subsequent updates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Kraken V4 Pro battery reporting + DeviceDetail Unknown state

Registry: mark Kraken V4 Pro (0x0568, 0x056c) as has_battery=true.

wrongly false because battery polling was not yet wired up, but the
hardware capability must be reflected accurately in the protocol layer.

a green ring before this fix.

only when hasCapability(device, 'BatteryReporting') is true,
matching the DeviceCard guard added in the previous commit.

Updated test fixtures in App.battery.mockipc.test.tsx to include
BatteryReporting in capabilities — matching what the daemon actually
sends for battery-capable devices.

fix: lighting txn_id and hide battery ring for no-battery devices

Bug 1 — Lighting: lighting_params() was falling through to the
DeathAdder catch-all (0x3F, LED_BACKLIGHT) for both BlackWidow
V3 Mini variants. Added explicit arms:
  - BlackWidowV3MiniHyperSpeedWired   → (0x1F, LED_BACKLIGHT)
  - BlackWidowV3MiniHyperSpeedWireless → (0x9F, LED_BACKLIGHT)
Ref: razerkbd_driver.c lines ~2107 and ~2123.

Bug 2 — Battery '?': DeviceCard rendered the battery ring for
every device, showing '?' for wired keyboards (no battery).
Now checks hasCapability(device, 'BatteryReporting') and only
renders the ring when the device actually has a battery.

TDD: 2 new lighting_params tests (device_manager.rs), 1 new
UI test for wired keyboard with no BatteryReporting capability.
Updated headset and mouse test fixtures to include
